text.title: Lesson Capture: Schema, Triggers, Specificity Rules
text.summary: Rules for capturing lessons with sufficient specificity to be actionable, including the required schema and capture timing.

section.title: When to Capture

rule.statement: Capture lessons within 24 hours of the trigger event. Weekly retros lose specifics; project-close retros lose everything from earlier phases.
rule.rationale: Memory decays exponentially. Within 24 hours, the context, numbers, and causal chain are still accessible; a month later only the emotion remains.

section.p: Capture triggers: after each milestone, when problems occur, when successes happen, during retrospectives, at project close.

section.title: Lesson Schema

rule.statement: Every lesson must have all five fields: situation (facts), impact (effect on project), root_cause (why), lesson (insight), recommendation (actionable change with a verb, an owner type, and a measurable outcome).
rule.rationale: Missing any field makes the lesson unusable: no situation = no context, no recommendation = no change.

section.reference: Validator that rejects incomplete or vague lessons

example.note: Lesson: "Communication was bad." No situation, no root cause, no actionable recommendation. This is the median entry in most lessons databases.

Situation: new framework introduced with no training time budgeted. Impact: development 40% longer. Root cause: excitement about new tech, no training estimate. Lesson: new technology requires explicit ramp time. Recommendation: add 25% buffer when introducing a framework new to the team and schedule a dedicated 2-day training sprint before development begins.

rule.statement: Reject any recommendation that lacks an action verb, an owner type, and either a metric or a process change. "Improve estimation" fails; "Add 25% buffer to estimates when introducing a framework new to the team" passes.
rule.rationale: Vague recommendations are re-logged identically on the next project. Specificity is what makes the lesson transferable.

rule.statement: Tag lessons by context, not just category: technology stack, team size, vendor involvement, regulated domain. Retrieval needs context match, not just topic match.
rule.rationale: A lesson about API integration on a regulated healthcare project is not the same as one on an unregulated internal tool, even if both are tagged "technical".

antipattern.description: Lessons captured only at project end.
antipattern.reason: Recency bias and survivorship bias dominate: only the last few weeks are memorable, and only people still on the project participate.

antipattern.description: Blame sessions framed as lessons-learned meetings.
antipattern.reason: Teams stop sharing honestly when the session becomes a post-mortem of individual failures. Facilitator must enforce "process not people" ground rule actively.

antipattern.description: LLM summarisation of retro notes producing generic platitudes.
antipattern.reason: Summarisation compresses two distinct lessons into one homogeneous statement. Preserve verbatim quotes alongside derived summaries.
